unit.name: Character-level language modeling and interpretable cells
unit.description: Shows character-level RNN language modeling, embeddings, sampling, and interpretable hidden activations.
unit.summary: A character-level language model is presented as a practical RNN example and an early ancestor of current language models [ts=2194s] [ts=2208s]. Characters can be one-hot encoded, the RNN updates hidden layers from the current input and previous hidden state, and the output predicts the next character via scores or softmax, making the task time-step-wise classification [ts=2221s] [ts=2242s] [ts=2280s]. At test time, characters are sampled one at a time and fed back into the model [ts=2294s]. Practical models use embedding layers: a learned matrix row is selected as the input vector, which optimizes better than passing sparse one-hot vectors directly [ts=2320s] [ts=2414s]. The lecture cites min-char-rnn.py as a 112-line implementation that generates increasingly coherent Shakespeare or code as training progresses [ts=2383s] [ts=2462s] [ts=2505s]. Greedy decoding always picks the maximum-probability output, so practical generation samples from the softmax distribution or uses beam search [ts=2626s] [ts=2677s]. Some RNN hidden cells become interpretable, tracking quotes, line length, if-statements, comments, or code depth [ts=2741s] [ts=2862s].

key_points.text: Character-level RNN language modeling predicts the next character at each time step.
Generated characters are fed back into the RNN at test time.
Embedding layers select a learned vector row instead of using raw one-hot inputs.
Greedy decoding can repeat the same output; sampling or beam search gives alternatives.
RNN cells can track interpretable features such as quotes, if-statements, and code depth.

kp_catalog.name: Character-level autoregressive language modeling
kp_catalog.description: Predicting the next character/token, feeding generated outputs back in, and sampling from probabilities.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because character-level autoregressive language modeling clarifies predicting the next character/token, feeding generated outputs back in, and sampling from probabilities, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices.

kp_catalog.name: Recurrent neural networks
kp_catalog.description: Shared-parameter sequence models that update a hidden state over time to summarize prior context. Updating a hidden state from current input and previous hidden state at every time step.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because recurrent neural networks formalizes how shared-parameter sequence models that update a hidden state over time to summarize prior context, which later units use as core machinery rather than background context.
